document: A Comprehensive Guide to the Law of Confidence and Breach of Confidence in Singapore_ Insights from [2024] SGCA 16 - IRB Law Singapore (Singapore - Row 33)

sections.heading: Whether these claims could be made in respect of the same set of confidential
sections.title: Confidential information claims: mutual exclusivity of wrongful gain and loss claims
sections.summary: This section clarifies that claims for wrongful gain and wrongful loss regarding the same set of confidential information are mutually exclusive. The court's application of the Coco test prevents concurrent claims under different legal frameworks, ensuring no double recovery. Plaintiffs must precisely delineate claims to distinct information sets to avoid overlapping claims and maintain clear burden of proof requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section matters for DTIAs as it establishes that claims for the same confidential information cannot be split into wrongful gain and loss claims, requiring careful delineation of data sets to avoid overlapping legal claims during cross-border data transfers involving sensitive information.

requirements.text: Claims for wrongful gain and wrongful loss regarding the same set of confidential information are mutually exclusive
The Coco test precludes concurrent application of the I-Admin test for the same information
Plaintiffs must clearly delineate claims for distinct sets of information

sections.source_quote: 2. Broad Issue: The court found that claims for wrongful gain and wrongful loss in respect of the same set of documents are mutually exclusive. The application of the Coco test precludes the concurrent application of the I-Admin test for the same information.
sections.relevance_reason: Tangential: focuses on legal claims, not direct data compliance
